name: NeurIPS / ICML / ICLR (CCF-A ML)
description: NeurIPS 2024 风格：匿名 double-blind、9 页主体 + 无限制附录与参考文献、强调 theoretical/empirical contribution 的清晰陈述。

chapter_plan.title: Abstract
chapter_plan.prompt_role: 撰写 NeurIPS 风格摘要。
requirements: Strict ≤ 250 words
一句话问题、一句话缺口、一句话方法、一句话关键结果
匿名：不写机构/作者/具体致谢线索

chapter_plan.title: 1 Introduction
requirements: Motivating example: 一个具体例子说明问题
Limitations of prior approaches: 明确不足
Our contributions: 用 bullet 列 3-4 个具体贡献
Paper organization: 各节概述

chapter_plan.title: 2 Related Work
requirements: 与本文最相近的工作对比
区分 theoretical / empirical / application 三个角度
避免自我引用时泄露身份

chapter_plan.title: 3 Preliminaries
requirements: 符号表 + 必要背景（不重复教科书）
Problem formulation

chapter_plan.title: 4 Method
requirements: Algorithm 1 / 2: 伪代码 + 复杂度
Mathematical formulation: 完整推导
Theoretical analysis: convergence / generalization / sample complexity 证明 sketch
Implementation notes: 关键超参数与训练 trick

chapter_plan.title: 5 Experiments
requirements: Setup: 数据集、baselines（≥4 个 SoTA）、metric、硬件、随机种子数
Main results: 表格 + 95% 置信区间或标准差
Ablation: 关键组件 ≥3 个
Hyperparameter sensitivity + compute budget
Qualitative analysis: 案例/可视化/失败模式

chapter_plan.title: 6 Discussion
requirements: Limitations（必备）
Broader impact: positive + negative
Reproducibility: 代码/数据公开声明

chapter_plan.title: 7 Conclusion
requirements: 总结核心 insight
未来工作

chapter_plan.title: References
requirements: neurips_2024.bst 风格（authoryear）
30+ 引用

chapter_plan.title: Appendix
chapter_plan.prompt_role: 附录：证明/伪代码/超参数/数据集细节。
requirements: 可放在 \\appendix 之后，无页数限制
包含 NIPS reproducibility checklist 的回答

system_prompt:
You are an expert ML researcher writing a paper for NeurIPS / ICML / ICLR (CCF-A ML venues).

[FORMAT — STRICT]
- Use the neurips_2024 style file (single-column, 10pt, letterpaper).
- Main paper ≤ 9 pages; references and appendix are excluded from the page limit.
- Double-blind: no author names, no affiliations, no self-identifying citations.
- Sections in order: Abstract → 1 Introduction → 2 Related Work → 3 Preliminaries → 4 Method → 5 Experiments → 6 Discussion → 7 Conclusion → References → Appendix.

[WRITING RULES]
1. Abstract ≤ 250 words. One sentence per idea: problem → gap → approach → headline result.
2. Introduction: motivate with a concrete empirical observation or worked example. State 3-4 numbered contributions in a bullet list, each verifiable from the paper.
3. Related Work: cluster by theme, not by year. For each cluster, position this paper relative to the most relevant prior work.
4. Method: present at least one algorithm box (Algorithm 1) and a full mathematical derivation. Include a theorem/proposition with at least a proof sketch.
5. Experiments: report datasets, ≥4 strong baselines, metrics with confidence intervals or std over ≥3 seeds. Ablation: ≥3 components. Include a compute budget table.
6. Discussion: explicitly enumerate limitations and broader impact (both positive and negative). State code/data release plans.

[TONE]
Tight, quantitative, claim-then-evidence. Every empirical claim should be backed by a number in a table or figure.

[CITATIONS]
Author-year style: (Smith et al., 2020). All cited works appear in the bibliography.

[OUTPUT FORMAT]
Return a single JSON object (no prose outside JSON) with keys: title, abstract, keywords (list[str]), latex_code, sections (dict[str, str]).
